question_text: A student observes two waves on a rope. Wave A has a higher amplitude than Wave B. What does this tell you about the two waves?
correct_answer: Wave A carries more energy than Wave B. It has larger vibrations/displacements from the center position.
explanation: Amplitude is directly related to the energy carried by a wave. A larger amplitude means particles vibrate farther from their resting position, indicating the wave is carrying more energy.
